summary.text: Provides extension methods for publishing container resources as container apps in Azure.

members.name: PublishAsAzureContainerApp
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<T> AzureContainerAppContainerExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerApp<T>(this IResourceBuilder<T> container, Action<AzureResourceInfrastructure, ContainerApp> configure)

summary.text: Publishes the specified container resource as a container app.

remarks.text: This method checks if the application is in publish mode. If it is, it adds the necessary infrastructure for container apps and applies the provided configuration action to the container app.

configure.text: The configuration action for the container app.

T.text: The type of the container resource.

summary.text: Provides extension methods for customizing Azure Container App resource.

members.name: ConfigureCustomDomain
members.signature: public static void ContainerAppExtensions.ConfigureCustomDomain(this ContainerApp app, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> customDomain, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> certificateName)

summary.text: Configures the custom domain for the container app.

children.text: extension method simplifies the process of assigning a custom domain to a container app resource when it is deployed. It has no impact on local development.
method is used in conjunction with the
callback. Assigning a custom domain to a container app resource is a multi-step process and requires multiple deployments.

method takes two arguments which are parameter resource builders. The first is a parameter that represents the custom domain and the second is a parameter that represents the name of the managed certificate provisioned via the Azure Portal
When deploying with custom domains configured for the first time leave the

parameter empty (when prompted by the Azure Developer CLI). Once the application is deployed successfully access to the Azure Portal to bind the custom domain to a managed SSL certificate. Once the certificate is successfully provisioned, subsequent deployments of the application can use this certificate name when the
For deployments triggered locally by the Azure Developer CLI the
path can by modified with the certificate name since Azure Developer CLI will not prompt again for the value.

remarks.text: This example shows declaring two parameters to capture the custom domain and certificate name and passing them to the

app.text: The container app resource to configure for custom domain usage.

certificateName.text: A resource builder for a parameter resource capturing the name of the certificate configured in the Azure Portal.

customDomain.text: A resource builder for a parameter resource capturing the name of the custom domain.

description.text: Throws if the container app resource is not parented to a

members.name: PublishAsAzureContainerAppJob
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<T> ContainerAppExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerAppJob<T>(this IResourceBuilder<T> resource, Action<AzureResourceInfrastructure, ContainerAppJob> configure)

summary.text: Allows configuring the specified compute resource as an Azure Container App Job.

remarks.text: This method adds the necessary infrastructure for container app jobs to the application builder and applies the specified configuration to the container app job. Note that the default trigger type for the job is set to Manual, and the default replica timeout is set to 1800 seconds (30 minutes).

configure.text: The configuration action for the container app job.

T.text: The type of the compute resource.

summary.text: Configures the specified compute resource as a manually triggered Azure Container App Job.

remarks.text: This is a convenience overload for the common case of manually triggered jobs. Manual trigger is the default trigger type, so this method provides a simpler API when no additional job configuration is needed.

T.text: The type of the compute resource.

members.name: PublishAsScheduledAzureContainerAppJob
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<T> ContainerAppExtensions.PublishAsScheduledAzureContainerAppJob<T>(this IResourceBuilder<T> resource, string cronExpression, Action<AzureResourceInfrastructure, ContainerAppJob>? configure = null)

summary.text: Configures the specified compute resource as a scheduled Azure Container App Job with the provided cron expression.

remarks.text: This method is a convenience wrapper around
that automatically configures the job with a schedule trigger using the specified cron expression.

configure.text: The configuration action for the container app job.

cronExpression.text: The cron expression that defines the schedule for the job.

T.text: The type of the compute resource.
